stemHtml: In Fly-By-Wire aircraft, when the overspeed function is active, it will...

options.a: disconnect the flight controls from the aerodynamic surfaces until the airspeed reduces below the trigger speed.
options.b: cause tactile feedback, such as vibration, to the flight controls and prevent further acceleration using flight control system inputs.
options.c: prevent a speed increase by over-riding manual or automatic control input, until the airspeed falls below the threshold speed.
options.d: require a constant nose-down control input in order to make an increase in speed.
